title: Schema title field is the authoritative source for block-kind display_name
context: Every .project/schemas/*.schema.json already carries title (e.g. "Framework Gaps" / "Design Decisions" / "Features") + description authored as canonical purpose statements. Authoring config.block_kinds[].display_name from scratch would duplicate this — and worse, drift if the two diverge. Schema title is authored once per block kind alongside required/properties/etc., already AJV-validated through normal schema-write surfaces. Config.block_kinds[].display_name role was ambiguous prior to this decision: amplify schema.title (duplicate) vs override (deviation only). User direction 2026-05-14 surfaced this in dialogue while reverse-engineering config draft.
decision: Schema title field is the canonical source for block-kind display_name. config.block_kinds[].display_name is override-only — populate ONLY when prose deviation from schema.title is needed for display context. Default rendering reads schema.title; UI/macros fall back to schema.title when config.block_kinds[].display_name absent. Same authority pattern extends to other block-kind metadata expressible in schema (description / title) before adding parallel config fields.
consequences: Reduces config.json authoring burden — 21 block_kinds[] entries don't each need display_name unless overriding.
Schema-side title edits propagate to display layer automatically.
Future display-name divergence requires explicit override entry in config; surfaces deviation as substrate.
Same principle extends to: schema.description → config.block_kinds[].description (if added); schema.properties[X].description → field-level display strings.
Phase 5 sub-phase 5.1 (config.json authoring) is reduced in scope — display_name layer mostly omittable.
